node.excerpt:
Lacedaemonians! the confidence which you feel in your
constitution and social order, inclines you to receive any reflections
of ours on other powers with a certain scepticism. Hence springs
your moderation, but hence also the rather limited knowledge which
you betray in dealing with foreign politics. Time after time was our
voice raised to warn you of the blows about to be dealt us by Athens,
and time after time, instead of taking the trouble to ascertain the
worth of our communications, you contented yourselves with
suspecting the speakers of…
